tags.notable_features: U-shaped horseshoe bay, anchor chains from whaling era, resident frogfish, multiple moray eel species including dragon eel, finger reef wall structures both sides of bay
tags.marine_life: Hawaiian green sea turtles (honu), dragon moray eels, snowflake moray, frogfish (resident), octopus, leaf scorpionfish, devil scorpionfish, hermit crabs, cone snails, spotted eagle rays (rare), Galapagos sharks (rare), Hawaiian monk seals (occasional), trumpet fish, triggerfish

tags.notable_features: Three partial lava tubes with archways and overhangs; site is located a few hundred yards offshore in front of the former Sheraton Kauai Resort; sandwiched between Koloa Landing to the west and Ice Box to the east
tags.marine_life: Hawaiian green sea turtles (consistently encountered, often 10+ per dive), whitemouth moray eels, tropical reef fish, octopuses, nudibranchs, white-tip reef sharks (occasional), manta rays (rare seasonal)

tags.notable_features: Horseshoe inlet with remains of steam schooner Pele (sank March 24, 1895 after striking sunken rock Kalanipuao); wreck remains include boiler, hull plates, and anchors; small lava tubes on either side of inlet; black coral stands; depths 65-90 feet (20-27m)
tags.wreck_cause: struck sunken rock Kalanipuao, three-quarters of a mile from shore between Koloa and Eleele
tags.wreck_cargo: 200 tons of coal and general freight
tags.marine_life: Whiskered boarfish (regular), white-tip reef sharks, sea turtles, octopus, lemon butterfly fish, milletseed butterflyfish, pennant butterflyfish, helmet conch, lobsters, large crabs, black coral, dolphins (frequent)

tags.notable_features: Broad lava shelf running parallel to southeast shore, shelf starts around 60 feet then drops off with a 15-foot overhang below; pink and white cauliflower coral on shelf top; vibrant orange cup coral on overhangs; frequently done as a drift dive; strong currents possible; furthest boat site from harbor
tags.marine_life: Green sea turtles, nudibranchs (numerous species), orange-banded cowry, pincushion sea stars, Triton's trumpet shells, hermit crabs, small crustaceans in cauliflower coral, black coral on wall face
tags.depth_note: Shelf begins at 60 feet; ledge and overhang 60-100 feet (18-30m)

tags.notable_features: Large arch at base of mooring festooned with orange cup coral and black coral; reef plateau at ~40 feet leads to drop-off with undercut ledges and swim-through holes; advanced due to potentially rough surface conditions and strong currents
tags.marine_life: Eels (multiple species), lobsters, longnose hawkfish (rare, confirmed), sponge crab, bicolor anthias, ghost shrimp, yellow hairy hermit crab, reef sharks

tags.notable_features: Inner and outer reef with wide channel between; outer reef offers more lava tubes and arches; coral reef comes within a few feet of low-tide shore; commercial dive operations prohibited by DLNR as of 2024; best May-September when north shore swells subside; dive at or near high tide
tags.marine_life: Hawksbill sea turtles, reef sharks, nudibranchs, manta rays (reported), peacock flounder, parrotfish schools, butterflyfish schools, moray eels, octopuses
tags.seasonal_note: Best May-September (calm north shore); avoid winter high surf; no commercial dive operations as of 2024

tags.notable_features: Multiple named sites in channel between Niihau and Lehua Crater: Vertical Awareness (pinnacle 45-300ft), Keyhole Drift to Lehua Gardens, Niihau Arches (sea caves and lava tubes), Neon Cave to Pyramid Point Drift; 8-hour charter, certified divers only, 15+ logged dives recommended; visibility often hundreds of feet; strong open-ocean currents possible
tags.marine_life: Sandbar sharks, grey reef sharks (schools of 30+ reported), Hawaiian monk seals (one of highest concentrations in Hawaii), manta rays and eagle rays (seen approximately every other charter), schooling butterflyfish, bait balls, endemic Tinker's butterflyfish, endemic red lionfish, bicolor anthias, long-handed spiny lobster, spinner dolphins (November-May whales common)
tags.depth_note: Most dives 45-105 feet (14-32m); Vertical Awareness pinnacle drops to 300 feet

tags.notable_features: 11-mile sunken fringing reef off Na Pali Coast; furthest offshore dive site in Hawaii; no moorings, always live boat drift dive; first dive 60-100 feet, second dive 45-80 feet on different drop point; massive black coral stalks; ancient coral with deep cracks; surface interval exploring Na Pali sea caves
tags.marine_life: Large barracuda, spotted eagle rays, grey reef sharks, white-tip reef sharks, java moray eels, rainbow runners, stripe-belly puffers, black surgeonfish, wringtail wrasse, hammerhead sharks (reported), giant trevally, ono (wahoo), spinner dolphins (nearly guaranteed), humpback whales (winter)
tags.depth_note: First dive 60-100 feet (18-30m); second dive 45-80 feet (14-24m)

tags.notable_features: Protective breakwater creates calm waters; large horseshoe-shaped bay with two distinct dive areas; also serves as boat launch for south shore charters May-September; facilities include restrooms, showers, picnic tables; no lifeguard
tags.marine_life: Sea turtles, moray eels, schooling reef fish; Fathom Five notes this site for its abundant marine life in protected bay conditions
tags.depth_note: Bay maximum approximately 50 feet (15m); shallow sections suitable for beginners

tags.notable_features: Rocky lava shore entry (booties recommended); shallow reef 3-21 feet; long swim to main reef from shore; rocky entry point; good visibility; not many fish nor much coral at main reef section
tags.marine_life: Hawaiian green sea turtles (main attraction, several often visible), whitemouth moray eels, trumpetfish, Picasso triggerfish (humuhumunukunukuapua'a), reef fish; limited coral coverage
